response.justification: The candidate is an experienced full-stack engineer with a long career, but the resume shows minimal alignment with the role's core requirements. The position demands deep JVM expertise, strong Java/Kotlin, and Spring ecosystem experience, plus genuine interest in IDE internals. The resume's dominant stacks are TypeScript/Node.js and DLang, with Java listed only as a skill and used briefly long ago (robotic arms, mobile orchestration). There is no evidence of Kotlin, Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or IDE/language-tooling work. The brief C/C++ stint on NI LabWindows/CVI is the closest IDE-adjacent experience but is over 20 years old and not JVM-related.
strengths: Long tenure on complex, large-scale systems and developer tooling
Some exposure to Java and low-level/performance work (DLang, C/C++)
Strong engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, testing, craftsmanship mindset
concerns: No demonstrated Kotlin or Spring ecosystem experience
No IDE internals or language-tooling work in recent roles
Primary modern stack (TS/Node) does not match JVM tooling focus

key_factors.factor: No IDE internals or language tooling experience
